var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Get all the data
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:72']
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:82']

# Create mappings
user_ids = set([u['user_id'] for u in users_2016])
business_map = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_map[b['business_id']] = b

# Improved category extraction function
def extract_categories(description):
    """Extract business categories from description text."""
    if not description:
        return []
    
    categories = []
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    # Pattern matching for category extraction
    patterns = [
        r'(?:services in|including|categories of|in the categories of)\s+([^.]+?)(?:\.|$)',
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, desc_lower, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            # Get the text containing categories
            text = match.group(1)
            
            # Split by commas and 'and' to get individual categories
            # Use regex to handle various separators
            parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', text)
            
            for part in parts:
                # Clean up the category name
                part = part.strip()
                
                # Remove quotes and other unwanted characters
                part = part.strip("'\"-")
                
                # Remove trailing punctuation
                part = part.rstrip('.;,')
                
                # Skip empty or very short/long strings
                if not part or len(part) < 3 or len(part) > 100:
                    continue
                
                # Fix common extraction issues
                # If it starts with "cluding" (from "including" split), skip
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    # Extract the actual category name after "cluding"
                    actual_part = part[7:].strip()
                    if actual_part:
                        categories.append(actual_part)
                elif part.startswith('and '):
                    # Remove leading "and"
                    actual_part = part[4:].strip()
                    if actual_part:
                        categories.append(actual_part)
                else:
                    categories.append(part)
            
            break  # Only process first matching pattern
    
    return categories

# Count reviews per category
category_counts = Counter()
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_refs = set()
unique_businesses = set()

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    
    # Check if review is from a 2016 user
    if review['user_id'] not in user_ids:
        continue
    
    if '_' in business_ref:
        business_num = business_ref.split('_')[1]
        business_id = f'businessid_{business_num}'
        
        unique_businesses.add(business_id)
        
        if business_id in business_map:
            matched_reviews += 1
            business = business_map[business_id]
            description = business.get('description', '')
            categories = extract_categories(description)
            
            for category in categories:
                category_counts[category] += 1
        else:
            unmatched_refs.add(business_ref)

# Get top 5 categories
top_5 = category_counts.most_common(5)

result = {
    'total_users_registered_2016': len(users_2016),
    'total_reviews_by_2016_users': len(reviews),
    'reviews_matched_to_businesses': matched_reviews,
    'unique_businesses_reviewed': len(unique_businesses),
    'businesses_in_sample': len(businesses),
    'unique_categories_found': len(category_counts),
    'top_5_categories_with_reviews': top_5,
    'unmatched_business_refs': len(unmatched_refs)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Get all the data
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:72']
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:10']
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:82']

# Create mappings
user_ids = set([u['user_id'] for u in users_2016])
business_map = {}
for b in businesses:
    business_map[b['business_id']] = b

# Improved category extraction function
def extract_categories(description):
    """Extract business categories from description text."""
    if not description:
        return []
    
    categories = []
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    # Pattern matching for category extraction
    patterns = [
        r'(?:services in|including|categories of|in the categories of)\s+([^.]+?)(?:\.|$)',
    ]
    
    for pattern in patterns:
        match = re.search(pattern, desc_lower, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            # Get the text containing categories
            text = match.group(1)
            
            # Split by commas and 'and' to get individual categories
            # Use regex to handle various separators
            parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', text)
            
            for part in parts:
                # Clean up the category name
                part = part.strip()
                
                # Remove quotes and other unwanted characters
                part = part.strip("'\"-")
                
                # Remove trailing punctuation
                part = part.rstrip('.;,')
                
                # Skip empty or very short/long strings
                if not part or len(part) < 3 or len(part) > 100:
                    continue
                
                # Fix common extraction issues
                # If it starts with "cluding" (from "including" split), skip
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    # Extract the actual category name after "cluding"
                    actual_part = part[7:].strip()
                    if actual_part:
                        categories.append(actual_part)
                elif part.startswith('and '):
                    # Remove leading "and"
                    actual_part = part[4:].strip()
                    if actual_part:
                        categories.append(actual_part)
                else:
                    categories.append(part)
            
            break  # Only process first matching pattern
    
    return categories

# Count reviews per category
category_counts = Counter()
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_refs = set()
unique_businesses = set()

for review in reviews:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    
    # Check if review is from a 2016 user
    if review['user_id'] not in user_ids:
        continue
    
    if '_' in business_ref:
        business_num = business_ref.split('_')[1]
        business_id = f'businessid_{business_num}'
        
        unique_businesses.add(business_id)
        
        if business_id in business_map:
            matched_reviews += 1
            business = business_map[business_id]
            description = business.get('description', '')
            categories = extract_categories(description)
            
            for category in categories:
                category_counts[category] += 1
        else:
            unmatched_refs.add(business_ref)

# Get top 5 categories
top_5 = category_counts.most_common(5)

result = {
    'total_users_registered_2016': len(users_2016),
    'total_reviews_by_2016_users': len(reviews),
    'reviews_matched_to_businesses': matched_reviews,
    'unique_businesses_reviewed': len(unique_businesses),
    'businesses_in_sample': len(businesses),
    'unique_categories_found': len(category_counts),
    'top_5_categories_with_reviews': top_5,
    'unmatched_business_refs': len(unmatched_refs)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Get the file path
businesses_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:94']
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Get reviews
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:100']

print('Loaded businesses count:', len(businesses))
print('Reviews count:', len(reviews))
print('First business:', businesses[0])
print('First review:', reviews[0])

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

# Get reviews by 2016 users

# Get businesses from the query result
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:114']  # This is a list

print('Sample businesses:')

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Get reviews by 2016 users
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:100']

# Get businesses from the query result
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:114']  # This is a list

print(f'Reviews: {len(reviews)}')
print(f'Businesses in sample: {len(businesses)}')

# Show some sample data
print('Sample reviews:')
for i, review in enumerate(reviews[:3]):
    print(f'  {i+1}. {review}')

print('Sample businesses:')
for i, business in enumerate(businesses[:3]):
    print(f'  {i+1}. {business}')

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter
import re

# Load all businesses from the file
businesses_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:120']
with open(businesses_file, 'r') as f:
    all_businesses = json.load(f)

# Get reviews by 2016 users and the user list
reviews_2016_users = locals()['var_functions.query_db:100']
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:96']

# Create mappings
user_ids_2016 = set([u['user_id'] for u in users_2016])
business_map = {b['business_id']: b for b in all_businesses}

# Category extraction function
def extract_categories(description):
    """Extract business categories from description text."""
    if not description:
        return []
    
    categories = []
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in A, B, and C."
    if 'services in' in desc_lower:
        start = desc_lower.find('services in') + len('services in')
        remaining = description[start:]
        if '.' in remaining:
            remaining = remaining[:remaining.find('.')]
        
        parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', remaining)
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
            if part and 3 < len(part) < 100:
                # Clean up common extraction artifacts
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    part = part[7:].strip()
                if part.startswith('and '):
                    part = part[4:].strip()
                if part and not part.startswith('including'):
                    categories.append(part)
    
    # Pattern 2: "including A, B, and C."
    elif 'including' in desc_lower:
        start = desc_lower.find('including') + len('including')
        remaining = description[start:]
        if '.' in remaining:
            remaining = remaining[:remaining.find('.')]
        
        parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', remaining)
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
            if part and 3 < len(part) < 100:
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    part = part[7:].strip()
                if part.startswith('and '):
                    part = part[4:].strip()
                if part and not part.startswith('including'):
                    categories.append(part)
    
    # Pattern 3: "categories of A, B, C."
    elif 'categories of' in desc_lower:
        start = desc_lower.find('categories of') + len('categories of')
        remaining = description[start:]
        if '.' in remaining:
            remaining = remaining[:remaining.find('.')]
        
        parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', remaining)
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
            if part and 3 < len(part) < 100:
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    part = part[7:].strip()
                if part.startswith('and '):
                    part = part[4:].strip()
                if part:
                    categories.append(part)
    
    return categories

# Count reviews per category
category_review_counts = Counter()
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_refs = set()
reviews_per_business = Counter()

# Process each review from 2016 users
for review in reviews_2016_users:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    
    if '_' not in business_ref:
        continue
    
    # Convert businessref_X to businessid_X
    business_num = business_ref.split('_')[1]
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_num}'
    
    reviews_per_business[business_id] += 1
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        matched_reviews += 1
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        categories = extract_categories(description)
        
        for category in categories:
            category_review_counts[category] += 1
    else:
        unmatched_refs.add(business_ref)

# Get top 5 categories
top_5_categories = category_review_counts.most_common(5)

# Prepare comprehensive results
result = {
    'total_users_registered_2016': len(users_2016),
    'total_reviews_by_2016_users': len(reviews_2016_users),
    'reviews_successfully_matched': matched_reviews,
    'reviews_could_not_match': len(unmatched_refs),
    'unique_businesses_reviewed': len(reviews_per_business),
    'businesses_in_database': len(all_businesses),
    'total_unique_categories_found': len(category_review_counts),
    'top_5_categories_with_most_reviews': [
        {'category': cat, 'review_count': count}
        for cat, count in top_5_categories
    ],
    'sample_unmatched_business_refs': list(unmatched_refs)[:5]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

user_ids_2016 = set([u['user_id'] for u in users_2016])
business_map = {b['business_id']: b for b in all_businesses}

# Category extraction function
def extract_categories(description):
    """Extract business categories from description text."""
    if not description:
        return []
    
    categories = []
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    # Pattern 1: "services in A, B, and C."
    if 'services in' in desc_lower:
        start = desc_lower.find('services in') + len('services in')
        remaining = description[start:]
        if '.' in remaining:
            remaining = remaining[:remaining.find('.')]
        
        parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', remaining)
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
            if part and 3 < len(part) < 100:
                # Clean up common extraction artifacts
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    part = part[7:].strip()
                if part.startswith('and '):
                    part = part[4:].strip()
                if part and not part.startswith('including'):
                    categories.append(part)
    
    # Pattern 2: "including A, B, and C."
    elif 'including' in desc_lower:
        start = desc_lower.find('including') + len('including')
        remaining = description[start:]
        if '.' in remaining:
            remaining = remaining[:remaining.find('.')]
        
        parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', remaining)
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
            if part and 3 < len(part) < 100:
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    part = part[7:].strip()
                if part.startswith('and '):
                    part = part[4:].strip()
                if part and not part.startswith('including'):
                    categories.append(part)
    
    # Pattern 3: "categories of A, B, C."
    elif 'categories of' in desc_lower:
        start = desc_lower.find('categories of') + len('categories of')
        remaining = description[start:]
        if '.' in remaining:
            remaining = remaining[:remaining.find('.')]
        
        parts = re.split(r',\s*|\s+and\s+|\&\s+', remaining)
        for part in parts:
            part = part.strip().rstrip('.').strip()
            if part and 3 < len(part) < 100:
                if part.startswith('cluding'):
                    part = part[7:].strip()
                if part.startswith('and '):
                    part = part[4:].strip()
                if part:
                    categories.append(part)
    
    return categories

# Count reviews per category
category_review_counts = Counter()
matched_reviews = 0
unmatched_refs = set()
reviews_per_business = Counter()

# Process each review from 2016 users
for review in reviews_2016_users:
    business_ref = review['business_ref']
    
    if '_' not in business_ref:
        continue
    
    # Convert businessref_X to businessid_X
    business_num = business_ref.split('_')[1]
    business_id = f'businessid_{business_num}'
    
    reviews_per_business[business_id] += 1
    
    if business_id in business_map:
        matched_reviews += 1
        business = business_map[business_id]
        description = business.get('description', '')
        categories = extract_categories(description)
        
        for category in categories:
            category_review_counts[category] += 1
    else:
        unmatched_refs.add(business_ref)

# Get top 5 categories
top_5_categories = category_review_counts.most_common(5)

# Prepare comprehensive results
result = {
    'total_users_registered_2016': len(users_2016),
    'total_reviews_by_2016_users': len(reviews_2016_users),
    'reviews_successfully_matched': matched_reviews,
    'reviews_could_not_match': len(unmatched_refs),
    'unique_businesses_reviewed': len(reviews_per_business),
    'businesses_in_database': len(all_businesses),
    'total_unique_categories_found': len(category_review_counts),
    'top_5_categories_with_most_reviews': [
        {'category': cat, 'review_count': count}
        for cat, count in top_5_categories
    ],
    'sample_unmatched_business_refs': list(unmatched_refs)[:5]
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Get data
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:100']
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:96']
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:114']  # This is a list of 5 businesses

# Load ALL businesses from the file we queried earlier
# The file should be accessible via the query result key
businesses_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:120']  # This should be the file path to 100 businesses

print('Businesses file path:', businesses_file_path)
print('Type:', type(businesses_file_path))

# Verify we can access it
with open(businesses_file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_businesses_data = json.load(f)

print(f'Successfully loaded {len(all_businesses_data)} businesses')
print(f'We have {len(reviews)} reviews by 2016 users')

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Get data
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:100']
users_2016 = locals()['var_functions.query_db:96']
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:114']  # This is a list of 5 businesses

# Load ALL businesses from the file we queried earlier
# The file should be accessible via the query result key
businesses_file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:120']  # This should be the file path to 100 businesses

print('Businesses file path:', businesses_file_path)
print('Type:', type(businesses_file_path))

# Verify we can access it
with open(businesses_file_path, 'r') as f:
    all_businesses_data = json.load(f)

print(f'Successfully loaded {len(all_businesses_data)} businesses')
print(f'We have {len(reviews)} reviews by 2016 users')

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

result.preview:
"Based on the analysis of 53 users who registered on Yelp in 2016 and their 44 reviews written since then, the 5 business categories that have received the most total reviews from these users are:

1. Shopping - 7 reviews
2. Food - 6 reviews
3. Restaurants - 6 reviews
4. Grocery - 6 reviews
5. American (New) - 5 reviews

All 44 reviews by 2016-registered users were successfully matched to businesses in the database, covering 31 unique businesses across 100 businesses in the dataset."
